label: Police Officer
note: The classic exam-gated uniformed job: ~24,000 officers, a flat $109,352 base; the ladder shows D/A detective tiers and managerial titles climbing to ~$182k.

label: Firefighter
note: One single competitive title, ~9,200 firefighters, all at the same $109,352 top base — a textbook exam-gated, flat-pay civil-service role.

label: EMT & Paramedic

ladder.title: SUPERVISING EMERGENCY MEDICAL SERVICE SPECIALIST-DPTY CHIEFS

note: A full exam-gated career ladder in one search: EMT ($49,047) to Paramedic to Supervising Specialist, all competitive titles.

label: Correction Officer
note: A single competitive title, ~8,000 officers at a $105,146 median — another exam-gated uniformed force with flat top pay.

label: Sanitation Worker
note: Exam-gated and surprisingly well paid: ~7,300 workers at a $92,093 median base, more than EMTs or school-safety agents.

label: School Safety Agent
note: An exam title inside the NYPD with the lowest band of the uniformed roles — ~4,000 agents at a $56,508 median.

label: Public-School Teacher
note: Wow caveat: DOE teachers are licensed, not civil-service-competitive, so they show as NO-EXAM here despite ~56,000 staff at a $110,848 median; per-session rows at the bottom are hourly-coded.

label: Administrative Staff Analyst
note: The widest range in the city: one exam title spanning $52 to $293,094 (median $128,122) — same title, a 5,600x base spread driven by part-time and senior managers.

label: Civil Engineer
note: A clean three-rung exam ladder: Intern to Assistant Civil Engineer to Civil Engineer ($116,808) — every rung competitive.

label: Accountant
note: Surprise: the base ACCOUNTANT title is NO-EXAM (median $76,423), while ADMINISTRATIVE ACCOUNTANT above it on the ladder IS exam-gated — same family, different gate.

label: Computer Systems Manager
note: High-paid tech that still requires an exam: ~1,400 managers, median $159,485, topping out near $284k.

label: Architect
note: A mixed ladder: licensed ARCHITECT and ADMINISTRATIVE ARCHITECT are exam titles, but SENIOR IT ARCHITECT (top of the list) is no-exam — exam status doesn't track pay.

label: Attorney / Agency Attorney

ladder.title: SPECIAL ASSISTANT TO THE DISTRICT ATTORNEY
CONFIDENTIAL ASST OFFICE OF DISTRICT ATTORNEY, KING COUNTY

note: The no-exam gap in one search: ZERO attorney titles appear on the Civil Service List, so all are no-exam — from Agency Attorney (median $108,350) up to District Attorney at $232,600.

label: Commissioner / Deputy Commissioner

ladder.title: COMMISSIONER OF DEPT OF INFO TECHNOLOGY & TELECOMMUNICATIONS

note: The high-paid no-exam wow: every commissioner title is appointed (no exam), topping out at $291,821 for agency heads; Deputy Commissioner median is $242,966.

label: City Planner
note: Counter-intuitive: despite being a policy/discretion job, City Planner (median $98,088) and Administrative City Planner ARE exam-gated competitive titles.

label: Administrative Engineer
note: A high-paid exam title: ~520 administrative engineers, median $156,639, the senior managerial rung of the competitive engineering ladder.
